lines.en_lit: spun of sky-blue” And derive “sky-blue” “sky-blue” from the curtain: Just as there, six so too here, six
Let us derive from its hem and pomegranates: Just as there, eight so too here, eight derive a utensil, from utensil, and not derive a utensil from an ornament of a utensil,
On the contrary, derive an object from that object itself, and not derive object from elsewhere. This is what we said to other garments about which shesh is not stated,
curtain twenty-four four each one of six there is neither judgment nor judge,

breastplate and ephod twenty-eight From where do we As it is written: “And you shall make a breastplate of judgment, the work of the skilled craftsman; like the work of the ephod you shall make it: Of gold, sky-blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen” four each of six twenty-four. gold four twenty-eight.
say gold six Rav Aḥa bar Ya’akov said the verse states: and cut it into cords, “Cord” “cords” Accordingly, there are four here.

Rav Ashi said: the verse states “To work it into the sky-blue, and into the purple” What we do? we make four of two each there would be eight. we make two of two each, and two of one each, “And you shall make,” that all its makings should be the same.

Raḥava said Rav Yehuda said: One who tears priestly vestments is flogged, as it is stated it shall not be torn” Rav Aḥa bar Ya’akov strongly objects to this: But perhaps this is what the Merciful One is saying An opening should be made in order that it not tear. Is it written: In order that it not be torn?

Rabbi Elazar said: One who detaches the breastplate from upon the ephod one who removes the staves of the Ark is flogged, as it is stated: shall not be detached they shall not be removed Rav Aḥa bar Ya’akov strongly objects to this: perhaps when the Merciful One said to strengthen them and make them fast so that not become detached and not be removed. Is it written: In order that it not become detached, and: In order that they not be removed?

Rabbi Yosei, son of Rabbi Ḥanina, raised a contradiction. it is written: “The staves shall be in the rings of the Ark; they shall not be removed from it” it is written: “And its staves shall be put into the rings” How is this could be removed did not come out.

That was also taught “The staves shall be in the rings of the Ark; might they should not move from their place the verse states: “And its staves shall be put into the rings” If And its staves shall be put, might they are inserted and removed the verse states: “The staves shall be in the rings of the Ark; How is this? They could be removed did not come out.

Rabbi Ḥama, son of Rabbi Ḥanina, said: What is which is written: acacia wood, standing” that stand in the direction in which they grew Alternatively, “standing” that they supported their plating Alternatively, “standing” Perhaps you will say their hope is lost and their chance is abandoned, the verse states “standing” that they stand forever and ever.
Rabbi Ḥama bar Ḥanina said: What is which is written: “The plaited serad garments, for serving in the Sanctuary”
